---
title: |
  Materials Genomics<br>Unit 8: Inverse Design and Optimization
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Materials Genomics"
---

## 01. Title: Inverse Design and Optimization
- Unit role in MG pipeline.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Formulate and solve inverse design tasks.

## 03. Recap from Unit 7
- Interpretation informs actionable design.

## 04. Forward vs inverse problems
- Prediction to prescription shift.

## 05. Design variable spaces
- Composition, process, structure knobs.

## 06. Objective function setup
- Single vs multi-objective goals.

## 07. Constraints in materials design
- Physics, manufacturability, safety.

## 08. Feasible region intuition
- Search space geometry.

## 09. Surrogate model role
- Fast objective approximation.

## 10. Gradient-based optimization
- When differentiability helps.

## 11. Gradient-free optimization
- Black-box settings.

## 12. Bayesian optimization preview
- Sample-efficient search.

## 13. Acquisition functions intuition
- Exploration vs exploitation.

## 14. Multi-objective optimization
- Pareto frontier concept.

## 15. Scalarization methods
- Weighted sums and tradeoffs.

## 16. Constraint handling strategies
- Penalty/barrier methods.

## 17. Robust optimization
- Uncertainty-aware design choices.

## 18. Optimization under uncertainty
- Risk-sensitive objectives.

## 19. Discrete + continuous variables
- Mixed-domain optimization.

## 20. Generative inverse design hint
- Latent-space search preview.

## 21. Active learning loop integration
- Experiment-query cycles.

## 22. Human-in-the-loop design
- Expert priors and overrides.

## 23. Avoiding shortcut designs
- Spurious optimum checks.

## 24. OOD candidate detection
- Trust region constraints.

## 25. Candidate diversity criteria
- Portfolio design logic.

## 26. Evaluation protocol
- Offline benchmark before lab.

## 27. Experimental validation planning
- Batch size and prioritization.

## 28. Cost-aware objective design
- Throughput and budget terms.

## 29. Fairness/ethics constraints (light)
- Resource-aware responsible design.

## 30. Reproducibility checklist
- Seeds, logs, candidate provenance.

## 31. Case sketch: alloy strength design
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 32. Case sketch: catalyst property target
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 33. Case sketch: battery material tradeoff
- Multi-objective scaffold.

## 34. Failure mode #1
- Surrogate exploitation artifacts.

## 35. Failure mode #2
- Constraint violations in top candidates.

## 36. Failure mode #3
- Collapse to low-diversity solutions.

## 37. Mitigation checklist
- Guardrails for robust design loops.

## 38. Visualization patterns
- Pareto fronts, acquisition maps.

## 39. Reporting standards
- Publish optimization protocol clearly.

## 40. Bridge to Unit 9
- Bayesian optimization deep dive.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: formalize objective
- Variables, constraints, metrics.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: train surrogate
- Baseline predictive model.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: run optimizer
- Generate candidate set.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: analyze tradeoffs
- Pareto and feasibility review.

## 45. Exercise scaffold: validation plan
- Rank candidates for experiments.

## 46. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 47. Summary table
- Methods vs assumptions.

## 48. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 49. Reading assignment
- Prepare active learning unit.

## 50. References
- Source list.

::: {#refs}
:::
